rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday November 16, 2018 line 1. Hearing Regarding Surplus Funds Holley Herman's claim is DENIED. Ms. Herman fails to provide any authority or evidence of entitlement to the distribution. The Court sets a hearing for December 14, 2018 for Ms. Herman's counsel of record to address why the Court should not issue an OSC Re: Why Counsel for Ms. Herman, Steve Gohari and Olympia LAW, P.C. shall not be sanctioned for making this claim in light of pending probate proceedings (issues with Probate Proceedings are addressed in the Petition placing counsel on notice). Response shall be filed and served no later that December 12, 2018. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday November 16, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT SPECIALIZED LOAN SERVICING, LLC, DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED without leave to amend. Plaintiff amended the first cause of action without leave of Court. The request for leave to amend this cause of action is DENIED as such amended cause of action will have the same fatal defects as currently alleged second cause of action as discussed below. The second cause of action fails as no facts supporting standing under UCL are alleged. Plaintiff did not address this argument in opposition and failed to offer any proposed facts to be alleged re: loss of money or property caused by the alleged wrongdoings of lack of information re: appeal and lack of specifics on reasons for denial of modification. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MIKE BUCKLEY ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A PUBLIC ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday November 16, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a statutory basis for the negligence cause of action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SH'TARA SEGOVIA VS. MARCIA A. THOMAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday November 16, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANT MARCIA THOMAS, JENNIFER DEBERRY, DPPM, INC. D/B/A/ ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Attorneys Fees GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Strike request for attorney's fees is DENIED: (a) motion to strike contract-based attorney's fees claim was withdrawn; (2) as to the CCP 1021.5 fees, Defendants fail to cite any cases where such request was disposed of at the pleading stage. Motion to Strike Request for Punitive Damages is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) to support assertions of malice, oppression, or fraud. All amendments shall be made in bold font. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRIS ROLIZ-MAGUIGAD VS. K5 FAMILY LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday November 16, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT K5 FAMILY LLC Amended Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike DENIED in part and Hearing Required in part. Motion is DENIED as to the 2nds and third causes of action. The purported cause of action for injunctive relief is not a cause of action and is not "a claim upon which relief may be granted." ANTI-SLAPP procedure is not applicable to requests for relied, such as requires for injunctive relief. Cause of action for Declaratory relief does not arise out of protected activity. It arises out of actual controversy regarding the enforceability of a contract. City of Alhambra v. D'Ausilio (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1301. Authority cited by the Defendant for the contrary position, Equilon Entreprises v. Consumer Cause, Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53 is distinguishable. Hearing Required as to the first cause of action for wrongful eviction. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on November 16, 2018 Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief containing only the following: (1) the specific wrongful act(s) by the defendant(s) that give rise to each claim within each cause of action; (2) line and page number in the complaint where such act is alleged (if such allegations appear in the complaint); and (3) relevant evidence in support of the specific act by Document title, page and line number, e. Plaintiff's Declaration, page 3, line 4. No further argument. Plaintiff may arrange the response in a 3 column The Court notes that vague references to "endeavors to recover possession" are not "acts." An example of wrongful "act" in a landlord-tenant context would be "entry by landlord without notice on date certain," "failure to fix a leaky faucet after notice of malfunction," etc. = (501/REQ)
